title: Fixing IndexError list index out of range in Synthesizer.generate_goldens_from_docs()
body:
This fixes https://github.com/confident-ai/deepeval/issues/1682

Explore the source code to identify the files and functions to modify.

step_plan.summary: Fix a variable-shadowing bug in Synthesizer.generate_goldens_from_contexts by renaming the inner-loop index so the outer context index is not overwritten, preventing IndexError when accessing source_files/_context_scores.

steps.operation: Rename inner-loop index variable to avoid shadowing outer context index and update its usages
steps.reason: The synchronous implementation currently uses `for i, context in enumerate(contexts):` as the outer loop and reuses `i` in an inner loop (`for i in range(max_goldens_per_context):`), which rebinds the outer context index. This causes later lookups like source_files[i] and _context_scores[i] to reference the wrong index (and sometimes be out of range). Renaming the inner loop variable (e.g., `k`) and adjusting any uses (such as in progress bar labels or local indexing that meant the per-golden index) prevents shadowing and preserves correct indexing into source_files and _context_scores.
steps.side_effects: Low-risk, localized change. Potential impacts: unit tests that assert exact progress-bar text or refer to the inner loop variable name could need updates (unlikely). No API changes — function signature unchanged. Async generation paths are unaffected because they already use a non-shadowing outer index name. Downstream code that relies on correct source_files/_context_scores mapping will now behave correctly; run/generate tests that cover synchronous generation with multiple contexts and max_goldens_per_context > 1 to confirm fix. If any local variables inside the inner loop previously used the name `k` or another chosen new name, ensure no naming collision when selecting the replacement variable name.
